sections.paragraph:
unused in pret. (in which tense there is used the form חַם from חָמַם, compare טֹוב fut. יִיטַב, רַע fut. יֵרַע), fut. יִחַם 1Ki 1:1, and יֵחַם (see the note), Deu 19:6 Eze 24:11 plur. יֶחֱמוּ for יֵחֲמוּ Gen 30:39 3 plural masc. יֵחַמְנָה (in the Chaldee and Arabic form for the common תֵּחַמְנָה, see Lehrgeb. p. 276) i.q. חָמַם to be hot (Arabic وحم to be hot, as the day; V. to be warm, of sexual desire in cattle). Eze 24:11 specially with wrath, Deu. loc. cit., and with sexual desire, hence to conceive (speaking of sheep), Gen 30:38, 39 Gen 30:39. [These two passages in Thes. are referred to חָמַם.] Impers. יֵחַם לֹו Ecc 4:11 and יִחַם לֹו 1Ki 1:1, to become hot. [See חָמַם .]

Note. Above at חָמַם I have followed the common arrangement, and referred the forms יֵחַם, יִחַם to the root יָחַם; however, let grammarians inquire, whether they should not all be referred to חָמַם; compare the form יֵחַמּוּ Hos 7:7.

Piel יִחַם 1. יֵחַם to be warm, with sexual desire, as cattle; to have sexual intercourse, Gen 30:41 31:10 . Hence to conceive, used also of a woman. Psa 51:7, וּבְחֵטְא יֶחֱמַתְנִי אִמִּי “and in sin did my mother conceive me.” יֶחֱמַתְנִי for יֵחֲמַתְנִי as אֶחֱרוּ for אִחֲרוּ, אֵחֲרוּ Jud. 5:28 .

Hence חֵמָה for יְחֵמָה.
